steps.title: Identify the clause structures
steps.content:
LEFT: Complete independent clause (“Cree director Chantelle Marie Anderson joined the growing number of Indigenous women adding their voices to North American cinema”).
RIGHT: Independent clause with “Her film” as subject (“Her film, a selection of the Native Women in Film Festival, is about a woman exploring her Métis identity”).

steps.title: Mark the sentence boundary
steps.content:
When two independent clauses appear consecutively, a period or semicolon must separate them.

Option C correctly applies this rule with a period. The other three violate it: B and D create comma splices; A produces an ungrammatical phrase.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
